mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising inflation and restrictive policy creates stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both series trend upward simultaneously.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression, or 20-day returns remain positive when both series increase concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real sales of new single-family homes and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, which expands corporate revenue streams and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during late-cycle expansion phases, or rolling 60-day return correlation with housing demand proxies falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect strong manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations when INDPRO increases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth falling below 100,000 per month indicates labor market cooling that reduces wage inflation expectations and consumer spending power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when payroll growth drops below the 100k threshold, or the threshold effect is not statistically distinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth combined with stable or declining unemployment rates confirms a synchronized economic expansion that boosts corporate profitability and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDP growth and unemployment stability shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or 60-day returns are negative when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing cycle, declining federal funds rates lower borrowing costs for corporations and stimulate risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days regardless of concurrent inflation readings.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during easing cycles, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with rate cuts in out-of-sample testing.
